rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned expressions.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only valid output features or declared auxiliary variables for assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the current annex scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
Validate that expression types match declared helper types to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation contracts in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements should relate visible inputs to visible outputs, not internal subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers in AGREE expressions; always ensure visibility or declaration.
Do not mix invalid or mismatched types in AGREE helper declarations and expressions.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level annexes where they are not valid.
